chapter_context.summary: Details the graphic allegory of two sisters, Oholah (Samaria) and Oholibah (Jerusalem), who lusted after Assyrian and Babylonian lovers and exceeded each other in lewdness, resulting in both being stripped and judged.

outline.text: Oholah (Samaria) and Her Assyrian Lovers and Ruin
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Oholah (Samaria) and Her Assyrian Lovers and Ruin (23:1-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Oholibah (Jerusalem) and Her Babylon and Egypt Lewdness
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Oholibah (Jerusalem) and Her Babylon and Egypt Lewdness (23:11-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Severity of Oholibah's Judgment: Drinking Deep Cup of Desolation
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Severity of Oholibah's Judgment: Drinking Deep Cup of Desolation (23:22-35), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Joint Indictment and Execution of the Adulterous Sisters
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Joint Indictment and Execution of the Adulterous Sisters (23:36-49), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 23, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
